mediumvisual

Mental Rotation Test

Test your spatial reasoning. Compare rotated shapes and determine if they match. Challenge your ability to mentally manipulate objects in 3D space.

VS
Are these the same shape?
0Score
1/10Round
0%Accuracy

Complete the game to see your score!

How to Take the Mental Rotation Test

1

Two shapes are shown. One may be rotated relative to the other.

2

Determine if the shapes are the same (just rotated) or different.

3

Click SAME if they match, DIFFERENT if they don't.

4

Complete 10 trials. Speed and accuracy both matter.

Tips to Improve Your Score

  • Imagine physically rotating the shape in your hands
  • Focus on one feature at a time and track how it changes
  • Use your hands to 'trace' the rotation in the air
  • Practice with real objects — rotate them and visualize the result

Frequently Asked Questions

What is mental rotation?

Mental rotation is the ability to imagine how an object would look when rotated in space. It's a key component of spatial intelligence and is used in navigation, engineering, architecture, and many scientific fields.

Are there gender differences in mental rotation?

Research has consistently found that on average, males perform slightly better on mental rotation tasks. However, with practice, these differences can be significantly reduced. Training and experience play a major role.

Can I improve my mental rotation skills?

Yes! Mental rotation is highly trainable. Playing video games (especially action and puzzle games), practicing with 3D puzzles, and regular spatial reasoning exercises can all improve your mental rotation ability.

About the Mental Rotation

The Mental Rotation is one of our most popular brain training exercises. Designed to test and improve your cognitive abilities, this game provides instant feedback on your performance.

Regular practice with this type of cognitive exercise can help improve your mental processing speed, accuracy, and overall brain health. We recommend practicing for 10-15 minutes daily for the best results.

Your scores are saved locally on your device, allowing you to track your progress over time. Challenge yourself to beat your personal best with each attempt!
